input.background: Table 5.1 lists the probability mass function (pmf) for the four patients illustrative pmf. The pmf satisfies the following properties: 1) All probabilities in the pmf are between 0 and 1. 2) Probabilities in the sample space sum to exactly 1. 3) The probability of a complement is equal to 1 minus the probability of the event.
input.question: Calculate the probability of $X=0$ or $X=1$, using the property that the probability of a union of disjoint events is the sum of their probabilities.
